summary: CRITICAL: Multi-year contract proration fails on mid-cycle plan change
description: Tenants with multi-year subscription contracts experience fatal errors when attempting to change plans mid-cycle. The proration engine fails to calculate prorated amounts correctly for contract periods exceeding 365 days. This was first reported by T-CORP-401 when attempting to upgrade their annual contract to a 2-year commitment. The error results in a 500 response and leaves the subscription in an inconsistent state requiring manual intervention.
resolution_details: Root Cause: The proration calculation used a hardcoded 365-day assumption for all contract periods. Multi-year contracts triggered integer overflow when calculating daily rates. Fix: Refactored proration engine to use BigDecimal for all monetary calculations and dynamically calculate daily rates based on actual contract length. Added comprehensive test coverage for 1-month, 1-year, 2-year, and 3-year contract scenarios. Deployment Impact: NONE (code-only change).
release_notes_excerpt: Fixes a critical issue where multi-year subscription contracts could not be modified mid-cycle due to proration calculation errors.

summary: CRITICAL: Stripe payment retry exhaustion causes incorrect charge reversal
description: When Stripe payment retries are exhausted after multiple failed attempts, the system incorrectly triggers a charge reversal instead of marking the payment as failed. This results in legitimate failed payments being treated as disputed charges, triggering refunds for subscriptions that should be suspended. First discovered when T-ALPHA-050 reported unexpected refunds for 12 subscriptions that should have been marked delinquent. Investigation revealed that retry exhaustion edge case was not properly handled in the payment state machine.
resolution_details: Root Cause: Retry exhaustion event was misinterpreted as a chargeback/dispute event due to shared error code handling. Fix: Separated retry exhaustion logic into distinct state transition. Added explicit check for retry count before triggering any refund workflows. Implemented idempotency keys for all Stripe refund operations to prevent duplicate refunds. Testing: Validated with 50+ retry exhaustion scenarios across development and staging environments. Deployment Impact: NONE (code-only change).
release_notes_excerpt: Resolves an issue where payment retry exhaustion could trigger unintended refunds instead of properly marking subscriptions as delinquent.

summary: CRITICAL: VAT calculation incorrect for EU cross-border transactions
description: For EU cross-border B2B transactions, the system incorrectly applies domestic VAT rates instead of implementing VAT reverse charge mechanism. This results in customers being overcharged and creates tax compliance issues. T-EURO-500 (German customer purchasing from UK entity) reported being charged 20% UK VAT instead of 0% under reverse charge rules. This affects all EU tenants conducting cross-border transactions and exposes them to tax audit risks.
resolution_details: Root Cause: Tax calculation service did not properly validate VAT registration numbers or implement reverse charge logic for qualifying B2B transactions. Fix: Integrated VIES VAT number validation API. Implemented reverse charge detection based on supplier/customer country combinations and valid VAT registration status. Added comprehensive tax scenario testing for all EU member states. Deployment Impact: HELM_CHANGE (new external API configuration for VIES integration).
release_notes_excerpt: Corrects VAT calculation for EU cross-border transactions to properly apply reverse charge mechanism, ensuring tax compliance.

summary: CRITICAL: Payment method deletion cascade fails, orphans active subscriptions
description: When a primary payment method is deleted while active subscriptions are still referencing it, the cascade deletion logic fails to properly reassign or suspend affected subscriptions. This leaves subscriptions in an orphaned state where renewal attempts fail silently. T-ULTRA-600 discovered this when 45 subscriptions failed to renew after their finance team rotated expired credit cards. No alerts were generated and subscriptions remained in 'active' status despite failed payment attempts.
resolution_details: Root Cause: Database foreign key constraint was set to ON DELETE SET NULL instead of using application-level cascade logic with proper state transitions. Fix: Implemented application-level payment method deletion workflow that: (1) Identifies all active subscriptions using the payment method, (2) Requires explicit reassignment or suspension decision, (3) Generates admin alerts for affected subscriptions, (4) Blocks deletion if active subscriptions exist without alternative payment method. Deployment Impact: DB_MIGRATION (foreign key constraint modification).
release_notes_excerpt: Prevents orphaned subscriptions by enforcing proper payment method reassignment or suspension before allowing payment method deletion.

summary: CRITICAL: Subscription cancellation doesn't trigger prorated refund for prepaid terms
description: When customers cancel subscriptions mid-cycle with 'cancel immediately' option, the system marks the subscription as canceled but fails to calculate and issue prorated refunds for the unused portion of prepaid billing periods. T-PRIME-700 reported that 8 customers who canceled quarterly subscriptions mid-term did not receive expected refunds, leading to customer satisfaction issues and chargeback disputes.
resolution_details: Root Cause: Cancellation workflow had two code paths (cancel at period end vs cancel immediately) but only the 'cancel at period end' path was integrated with the refund calculation service. Fix: Unified cancellation workflow to always calculate refund eligibility based on cancellation policy and prepaid balance. Added configurable cancellation policy support (full refund, prorated refund, no refund). Implemented automatic refund processing with customer notification. Deployment Impact: NONE (code-only change).
release_notes_excerpt: Ensures immediate subscription cancellations properly calculate and issue prorated refunds for unused prepaid periods.

summary: MAJOR: OAuth token refresh fails silently causing unexpected logouts
description: OAuth token refresh mechanism fails silently when refresh tokens expire, causing users to be logged out without warning during active sessions. Users report losing unsaved work and being redirected to login page unexpectedly. The issue affects approximately 5% of OAuth users based on error log analysis, particularly those with long-running sessions exceeding 7 days.
resolution_details: Root Cause: Token refresh error handling did not distinguish between transient network failures (retryable) and expired refresh tokens (requires re-authentication). Silent failures were logged but not surfaced to user. Fix: Implemented proper error classification with user-facing notifications for expired tokens. Added proactive token refresh 5 minutes before expiration. Implemented session persistence warning when refresh is about to fail. Added telemetry for token refresh success rates. Deployment Impact: NONE (code-only change).
release_notes_excerpt: Improves OAuth session handling by adding proactive token refresh and user notifications before session expiration.

summary: MAJOR: MRR calculation double-counts prorated subscription upgrades
description: Monthly Recurring Revenue (MRR) reports incorrectly double-count revenue when subscriptions are upgraded mid-cycle with proration. The proration credit is counted as separate revenue instead of being netted against the upgrade amount. This inflates MRR metrics by 3-8% for tenants with high upgrade activity. Finance teams using these reports for board presentations are receiving inaccurate revenue projections.
resolution_details: Root Cause: MRR aggregation query did not deduplicate proration events associated with the same subscription upgrade transaction. Both the original charge and proration adjustment were counted as separate revenue items. Fix: Refactored MRR calculation to group transactions by subscription_id and aggregate net revenue per billing cycle. Added transaction type filtering to exclude proration adjustments from standalone revenue counting. Implemented retroactive recalculation for past 6 months of MRR data. Deployment Impact: NONE (code-only change, data backfill via migration script).
release_notes_excerpt: Corrects MRR calculation to properly handle prorated subscription upgrades, preventing revenue double-counting in financial reports.

summary: MAJOR: Quota limits don't reset properly on subscription renewal
description: API quota limits fail to reset when subscriptions renew, causing customers to remain rate-limited even after their billing cycle resets. Multiple tenants reported that their API calls were being rejected with 429 errors despite their subscriptions renewing successfully. Cache invalidation for quota counters is not triggered by subscription renewal events, leaving stale quota data in Redis.
resolution_details: Root Cause: Subscription renewal event was not integrated with quota service cache invalidation workflow. Quota reset relied on TTL expiration which could lag renewal by up to 24 hours. Fix: Added event listener in quota service subscribing to subscription.renewed events. Implemented immediate cache flush for affected tenant's quota counters on renewal. Added quota reset confirmation in renewal success notification. Refactored quota TTL to align with subscription billing cycle end dates. Deployment Impact: NONE (code-only change).
release_notes_excerpt: Fixes quota limit reset timing to ensure API access is immediately restored when subscriptions renew.

summary: MAJOR: Webhook retry logic floods customer endpoints during outages
description: Webhook retry mechanism uses aggressive exponential backoff that floods customer endpoints with thousands of retry attempts when their systems experience brief outages. One tenant reported their API received 8000+ webhook requests in 30 minutes during a 5-minute maintenance window, causing their load balancer to trigger DDoS protection. Current retry logic does not implement proper circuit breaker pattern or maximum retry caps.
resolution_details: Root Cause: Webhook retry service used exponential backoff for delay timing but did not implement maximum retry limit per event. Each failed webhook spawned new retry jobs indefinitely until manual intervention. Fix: Implemented circuit breaker pattern with 5 retry attempts maximum and exponential backoff (2s, 10s, 60s, 300s, 1800s). Added endpoint health tracking that pauses all webhooks to consistently failing endpoints for 1 hour. Implemented webhook event batching for recovery scenarios reducing request volume by 80%. Added customer notification when webhooks are paused. Deployment Impact: NONE (code-only change).
release_notes_excerpt: Improves webhook reliability with circuit breaker pattern and retry limits to prevent overwhelming customer endpoints during outages.

summary: MAJOR: Credit notes don't correctly adjust tax amounts on partial refunds
description: When issuing partial refunds via credit notes, the tax adjustment calculation is incorrect for line-item-level refunds. The system recalculates tax on the refunded amount in isolation rather than proportionally reducing the original invoice tax. This creates tax reconciliation issues and incorrect accounting records. Affects approximately 15% of credit note transactions involving partial refunds with multiple tax rates.
resolution_details: Root Cause: Credit note generation cloned original invoice line items but recalculated tax independently instead of proportionally reducing original tax amounts. For mixed tax rate invoices (e.g., digital goods at 20% VAT, physical goods at 5% VAT), this produced incorrect net tax adjustments. Fix: Refactored credit note calculator to preserve original tax rate per line item and compute proportional reduction. Added validation comparing credit note total to original invoice ensuring credit never exceeds original charge. Implemented comprehensive test suite covering 25+ partial refund scenarios. Deployment Impact: NONE (code-only change).
release_notes_excerpt: Corrects tax calculation on credit notes to ensure accurate partial refund accounting across multiple tax rates.

summary: MAJOR: CRM integration stops syncing after OAuth token expires
description: Salesforce and HubSpot CRM integrations fail silently when OAuth tokens expire after 90 days. No error notifications are sent to customers and data sync simply stops working. T-LAMBDA-350 discovered their CRM data was 3 weeks stale before noticing the integration had failed. Token refresh logic exists but is not being triggered due to job scheduling bug.
resolution_details: Root Cause: Token refresh cron job was configured with incorrect timezone causing it to run during low-activity hours when no API requests were made. Without active requests, the refresh logic was never triggered. Expired tokens resulted in 401 errors that were caught and logged but not escalated. Fix: Refactored token refresh to run as scheduled job independent of API request activity. Implemented proactive token refresh 7 days before expiration with email notification to tenant admins. Added integration health monitoring dashboard showing last successful sync timestamp. Implemented automatic retry with admin notification on refresh failure. Deployment Impact: NONE (code-only change).
release_notes_excerpt: Improves CRM integration reliability with proactive OAuth token refresh and health monitoring to prevent silent sync failures.

summary: MAJOR: Invoice PDF generation causes memory leak for invoices with 100+ line items
description: Generating PDF invoices for large invoices (100+ line items) causes gradual memory exhaustion in the invoice-generator service. After processing 50-60 large invoices, the pod runs out of memory and crashes. Memory profiling revealed that PDF rendering library maintains in-memory canvas for each page without proper cleanup. This affects enterprise customers with usage-based billing generating detailed line-item invoices.
resolution_details: Root Cause: PDF generation library (wkhtmltopdf) loaded entire HTML document into memory before rendering. For large invoices with complex CSS, this created multi-GB memory allocations that were not released until process termination. Fix: Migrated to Puppeteer-based PDF generation with streaming rendering and page-level memory management. Implemented invoice pagination breaking invoices >50 line items into multiple pages with proper memory cleanup between pages. Added memory monitoring with automatic pod restart if memory exceeds 80% threshold. Reduced peak memory usage by 75% for large invoice rendering. Deployment Impact: HELM_CHANGE (new Chromium dependency and resource limit adjustments).
release_notes_excerpt: Resolves memory leak in PDF invoice generation for large invoices, improving system stability and performance.

summary: MINOR: Profile page mobile layout broken on iOS Safari
description: User profile management page renders incorrectly on iOS Safari with layout elements overlapping and form fields extending beyond screen boundaries. Issue is specific to iOS Safari and does not occur on Chrome mobile or Android browsers. Affects approximately 8% of mobile users based on browser analytics.
resolution_details: Root Cause: CSS flexbox implementation used viewport width units (vw) that iOS Safari interprets differently than other browsers, particularly with regard to the browser chrome. Safe area insets were not respected causing overlap with system UI. Fix: Replaced viewport units with percentage-based widths and added iOS-specific safe area CSS variables. Implemented CSS Grid for more predictable mobile layouts. Tested across iOS 15, 16, 17 on various iPhone models. Deployment Impact: NONE (CSS-only change).
release_notes_excerpt: Fixes mobile layout issues on iOS Safari for improved user profile management experience.

summary: MINOR: Email template formatting issues with long plan names
description: Transactional email templates do not handle long subscription plan names gracefully, causing text overflow and layout breaking in renewal reminder emails. Plan names exceeding 50 characters break onto multiple lines incorrectly or get truncated without ellipsis. Primarily a cosmetic issue affecting professional email appearance.
resolution_details: Root Cause: Email templates used fixed-width table cells for plan name display without text wrapping or truncation logic. HTML email constraints (limited CSS support) made responsive text handling challenging. Fix: Implemented CSS word-break properties compatible with major email clients. Added server-side truncation at 45 characters with ellipsis for plan names in email context. Created email template testing suite validating rendering across 15 email clients including Outlook, Gmail, Apple Mail. Deployment Impact: NONE (template-only change).
release_notes_excerpt: Improves email formatting to properly handle long subscription plan names across all email clients.

summary: MINOR: API usage chart shows incorrect timezone for UTC-7 and UTC-8 users
description: API usage analytics dashboard displays usage timestamps in UTC regardless of user's configured timezone. For users in Pacific timezone (UTC-7/UTC-8), this causes usage spikes to appear at incorrect hours making it difficult to correlate usage with business activities. Data is correct but visualization timezone is wrong.
resolution_details: Root Cause: Chart component received UTC timestamps from backend but did not apply client-side timezone conversion before rendering. User timezone preference was stored but not utilized in chart rendering logic. Fix: Implemented timezone-aware chart rendering using moment-timezone library. Added timezone selector in dashboard settings allowing users to view usage in any timezone. Chart tooltips now show both local time and UTC for clarity. Deployment Impact: NONE (frontend-only change).
release_notes_excerpt: API usage charts now correctly display timestamps in user's local timezone for accurate usage pattern analysis.

summary: PATCH: RBAC permission label typo in admin UI
description: Role-based access control (RBAC) permission management UI displays typo for 'subscription:write' permission, shown as 'subscrption:write'. Purely cosmetic issue affecting admin user experience. Underlying permission logic and functionality work correctly.
resolution_details: Root Cause: Typographical error in i18n translation file for permission labels. Fix: Corrected typo in en-US.json translation file. Verified all other permission labels for similar issues. No functional changes required. Deployment Impact: NONE (translation file update).
release_notes_excerpt: Corrects minor typographical error in admin permission labels.

summary: PATCH: API documentation portal broken anchor links
description: API documentation portal contains broken anchor links in table of contents navigation. Clicking TOC links results in 404 errors instead of jumping to the corresponding documentation section. Affects approximately 20% of documentation anchor links, primarily in webhook and authentication sections. Users can still access content by scrolling but navigation is inconvenient.
resolution_details: Root Cause: Documentation generator used outdated slug generation algorithm that did not match the anchor ID format used in rendered HTML. Inconsistent heading level transformations caused ID mismatches. Fix: Updated documentation build pipeline to use consistent slug generation across TOC and content sections. Implemented automated link validation in CI/CD pipeline to catch future anchor link regressions. Regenerated all documentation with corrected anchor links. Deployment Impact: NONE (static documentation rebuild).
release_notes_excerpt: Fixes navigation issues in API documentation portal by correcting broken anchor links.
